clusters.title: Trump pressures congressional Republicans by delaying landmark housing bill over voter ID law
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump cancels housing bill signing, demands US voter ID law first
bullet_summary: Trump said he will not sign the legislation until the SAVE America Act is passed
Reported by France24, CBS News, Financial Times and others

items.title: Trump pressures congressional Republicans by delaying landmark housing bill over voter ID law
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump announced Wednesday that he would not sign a new landmark housing bill until congressional Repulicans pass his proposed SAVE America Act to restrict voting procedures which he has been calling for for months.
items.content: US President Donald Trump announced Wednesday that he would not sign a new landmark housing bill until congressional Repulicans pass his proposed SAVE America Act to restrict voting procedures which he has been calling for for months.

items.title: Trump has testy meeting with GOP senators, telling Cassidy to sit down
items.standfirst: President Trump met with Republican senators soon after canceling plans to sign bipartisan housing affordability legislation at the Capitol.
items.content: President Trump met with Republican senators soon after canceling plans to sign bipartisan housing affordability legislation at the Capitol.

items.title: Trump meets with GOP senators after refusing to sign housing bill
items.standfirst: President Trump's decision to abruptly cancel the signing of a landmark bipartisan housing bill marked the latest misalignment between him and GOP lawmakers.
items.content: President Trump's decision to abruptly cancel the signing of a landmark bipartisan housing bill marked the latest misalignment between him and GOP lawmakers.

items.title: Democratic lawmakers slam Trump for not signing housing act
items.standfirst: Democratic congressional leaders spoke Wednesday after President Trump canceled a signing ceremony for a landmark housing affordability bill that passed Congress by wide bipartisan margins. Trump said he will not sign the legislation until the SAVE America Act is passed.
items.content: Democratic congressional leaders spoke Wednesday after President Trump canceled a signing ceremony for a landmark housing affordability bill that passed Congress by wide bipartisan margins. Trump said he will not sign the legislation until the SAVE America Act is passed.

items.title: Trump cancels housing bill signing, reiterates demand for SAVE America Act
items.standfirst: President Trump canceled a planned signing ceremony on Wednesday for a housing affordability bill that passed Congress by wide bipartisan margins.
items.content: President Trump canceled a planned signing ceremony on Wednesday for a housing affordability bill that passed Congress by wide bipartisan margins.

items.title: Trump scraps signing of US housing affordability bill
items.standfirst: Bipartisan legislation is designed to boost supply and curb institutional investors’ role in property market
items.content: Bipartisan legislation is designed to boost supply and curb institutional investors’ role in property market

items.title: Trump cancels housing bill signing, demands US voter ID law first
items.standfirst: The affordable housing bill had been passed by bipartisan support, a rare move in a deeply divided US Congress.
items.content: The affordable housing bill had been passed by bipartisan support, a rare move in a deeply divided US Congress.

clusters.title: U.N. nuclear agency chief says Iran inspections will happen, at some point
clusters.neutral_headline: UN nuclear chief says Iran inspections will happen, Tehran says after deal
bullet_summary: Head of the IAEA, Rafael Grossi, said the MoU stated 'explicitly' that the agency would supervise inspections in Iran
's atomic agency said Iran's nuclear enrichment sites would be inspected as part of the interim deal

items.title: U.N. nuclear agency chief says Iran inspections will happen, at some point
items.standfirst: After conflicting remarks from Iran and the U.S., the U.N. nuclear agency chief says Iranian sites will be inspected, but the timing is "not essential."
items.content: After conflicting remarks from Iran and the U.S., the U.N. nuclear agency chief says Iranian sites will be inspected, but the timing is "not essential."

items.title: UN nuclear chief says Iran inspections will happen, Tehran says after deal
items.standfirst: Head of the IAEA, Rafael Grossi, said the MoU stated 'explicitly' that the agency would supervise inspections in Iran.
items.content: Head of the IAEA, Rafael Grossi, said the MoU stated 'explicitly' that the agency would supervise inspections in Iran.

items.title: UN nuclear chief says inspectors will visit Iran sites as part of war deal
items.standfirst: Rafael Grossi says the IAEA is "working on modalities" but an Iranian minister says such access would only be part of a final deal with the US.
items.content: Rafael Grossi says the IAEA is "working on modalities" but an Iranian minister says such access would only be part of a final deal with the US.

items.title: U.N. nuclear chief says inspectors will visit Iran, but Iran says only after final deal
items.standfirst: The head of the U.N.'s atomic agency said Iran's nuclear enrichment sites would be inspected as part of the interim deal. Iran insists any such visit would only come after a final deal.
items.content: The head of the U.N.'s atomic agency said Iran's nuclear enrichment sites would be inspected as part of the interim deal. Iran insists any such visit would only come after a final deal.(Image credit: Kyodo News)

items.content: Presidential candidate Ivan Cepeda accepted the victory of his opponent Abelardo de la Espriella.

items.title: Colombia's left-wing presidential candidate concedes defeat
items.standfirst: Senator Iván Cepeda was beaten by right-wing businessman Abelardo de la Espriella by less than a percentage point.
items.content: Senator Iván Cepeda was beaten by right-wing businessman Abelardo de la Espriella by less than a percentage point.

items.title: Colombia’s leftwing candidate concedes election to Trump-endorsed millionaire
items.standfirst: Leftist Iván Cepeda conceded to far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella who won by razor-thin marginThe defeated leftwing candidate in Colombia’s presidential runoff has conceded to the far-right, Trump-admiring millionaire lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella.Since Sunday night, the preliminary count had already pointed to a De la Espriella victory by a razor-thin margin of less than 1% of the vote. Continue reading...

items.title: Colombia's left-wing candidate Cepeda concedes to De la Espriella in presidential election
items.standfirst: Colombia's left-wing candidate Ivan Cepeda on Wednesday conceded electoral defeat to right-wing rival Abelardo de la Espriella after Sunday's second-round presidential vote.
items.content: Colombia's left-wing candidate Ivan Cepeda on Wednesday conceded electoral defeat to right-wing rival Abelardo de la Espriella after Sunday's second-round presidential vote.

clusters.title: Mamdani says progressive primary victories show hunger "for a new kind of politics"
clusters.neutral_headline: Clean sweep for Mamdani-backed candidates in New York's Democratic primary
bullet_summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani said the progressive message to the average person is driving wins in Democratic primaries in his state
Reported by CBS News, France24, BBC World and others

items.title: Mamdani says progressive primary victories show hunger "for a new kind of politics"
items.standfirst: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani said the progressive message to the average person is driving wins in Democratic primaries in his state. On Capitol Hill, House Speaker Mike Johnson shared his reaction.
items.content: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani said the progressive message to the average person is driving wins in Democratic primaries in his state. On Capitol Hill, House Speaker Mike Johnson shared his reaction.

items.title: Mamdani-backed candidates win New York primaries
items.standfirst: Three candidates backed by New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani won Democratic primary elections for seats in the US House of Representatives on Tuesday, according to US media projections, an outcome that showcased Mamdani's rising influence on the Democratic party.
items.content: Three candidates backed by New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani won Democratic primary elections for seats in the US House of Representatives on Tuesday, according to US media projections, an outcome that showcased Mamdani's rising influence on the Democratic party.

items.title: Clean sweep for Mamdani-backed candidates in New York's Democratic primary
items.standfirst: Brad Lander unseats Dan Goldman in a race that laid bare the city's divisions over the Israel-Gaza war.
items.content: Brad Lander unseats Dan Goldman in a race that laid bare the city's divisions over the Israel-Gaza war.

items.title: Congress passes major housing bill. And, Mamdani-backed candidates sweep NYC primaries
items.standfirst: Congress has passed the largest housing affordability bill in decades. And all three candidates endorsed by New York City mayor Zohran Mamdani have won their primaries.
items.content: Congress has passed the largest housing affordability bill in decades. And all three candidates endorsed by New York City mayor Zohran Mamdani have won their primaries. (Image credit: Heather Diehl)

items.title: Mamdani influences New York City's congressional primaries with 3 big wins
items.standfirst: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani endorsed three left-of-center candidates in the congressional Democratic primaries, and all three are set to win, CBS News projects.
items.content: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani endorsed three left-of-center candidates in the congressional Democratic primaries, and all three are set to win, CBS News projects.

clusters.title: France reports Ebola case in doctor returning from Congo
bullet_summary: The patient was transferred to a specialist facility and was in a stable condition, the ministry said in a statement
France has reported its first case of Ebola during the latest outbreak after a doctor returning from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) tested positive

items.title: France reports Ebola case in doctor returning from Congo
items.standfirst: A doctor who recently returned ​to France from a humanitarian mission in the Democratic Republic of Congo has tested positive for Ebola, marking the country's ​first confirmed ‌case linked to the current ⁠outbreak, the health ministry said on Wednesday.
items.content: A doctor who recently returned ​to France from a humanitarian mission in the Democratic Republic of Congo has tested positive for Ebola, marking the country's ​first confirmed ‌case linked to the current ⁠outbreak, the health ministry said on Wednesday.

items.title: France confirms first Ebola case
items.standfirst: The French doctor had been working in DR Congo, where more than 260 people are known to have died.
items.content: The French doctor had been working in DR Congo, where more than 260 people are known to have died.

items.title: France confirms first Ebola case in doctor who had worked in DRC
items.standfirst: French health ministry says patient’s contacts are being traced and that risk to European public is very lowThe first case of Ebola has been confirmed in France, the country’s health ministry has said, in a doctor who had returned from a humanitarian mission to an area affected by the outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo.The patient was transferred to a specialist facility and was in a stable condition, the ministry said in a statement. “All precautionary measures, including the patient’s isolation, were taken upon his arrival in the country, with transfer to the hospital under secure conditions to prevent any risk of contamination.” Continue reading...
items.content: French health ministry says patient’s contacts are being traced and that risk to European public is very lowThe first case of Ebola has been confirmed in France, the country’s health ministry has said, in a doctor who had returned from a humanitarian mission to an area affected by the outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo.The patient was transferred to a specialist facility and was in a stable condition, the ministry said in a statement. “All precautionary measures, including the patient’s isolation, were taken upon his arrival in the country, with transfer to the hospital under secure conditions to prevent any risk of contamination.” Continue reading...

items.title: France reports first Ebola case
items.standfirst: France has reported its first case of Ebola during the latest outbreak after a doctor returning from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) tested positive.
items.content: France has reported its first case of Ebola during the latest outbreak after a doctor returning from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) tested positive.

clusters.title: France sees hottest day on record Wednesday
clusters.neutral_headline: Power outages hit France as it records hottest day since measurements began
bullet_summary: France on Wednesday experienced its hottest day since measurements began in 1947, the national weather agency said, breaking a record set just a day earlier
More than 50 departments across mainland France recorded temperatures of 40C or higher since the latest heatwave began on June 17, an AFP analysis of weather data showed Wednesday
“It is possible we could see temperatures higher than the 39C if the final values are at the upper end of our narrow range,” he said, according to the Press Association

items.title: France sees hottest day on record Wednesday
items.standfirst: France on Wednesday experienced its hottest day since measurements began in 1947, the national weather agency said, breaking a record set just a day earlier. More than 50 departments across mainland France recorded temperatures of 40C or higher since the latest heatwave began on June 17, an AFP analysis of weather data showed Wednesday. FRANCE 24's James Andre reports.
items.content: France on Wednesday experienced its hottest day since measurements began in 1947, the national weather agency said, breaking a record set just a day earlier. More than 50 departments across mainland France recorded temperatures of 40C or higher since the latest heatwave began on June 17, an AFP analysis of weather data showed Wednesday. FRANCE 24's James Andre reports.

items.title: Power outages hit France as it records hottest day since measurements began
items.standfirst: France has recorded it hottest day since records began in 1947, its national weather agency says, breaking a record set on Tuesday.
items.content: France has recorded it hottest day since records began in 1947, its national weather agency says, breaking a record set on Tuesday.

items.title: Europe heatwave live: UK breaks temperature record for June as parts of France hit 40C – as it happened
items.standfirst: West Sussex reports temperature of 35.8C, beating previous record from 1976; red weather alert extended to 72 of France’s 96 mainland departmentsGrahame Madge, a Met Office spokesperson, said the agency is forecasting 39C as a headline maximum temperature on Thursday in the UK, most likely for somewhere in London or the south-east.“It is possible we could see temperatures higher than the 39C if the final values are at the upper end of our narrow range,” he said, according to the Press Association. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Trains halted across Germany by communication system problem

items.title: Trains halted across Germany by communication system problem
items.standfirst: A nationwide failure in Deutsche Bahn’s GSM-R rail communications system briefly stopped all trains across Germany, stranding passengers and forcing delays at stations. Service later resumed step by step after the outage was resolved. [apnews.com]
items.content: A nationwide failure in Deutsche Bahn’s GSM-R rail communications system briefly stopped all trains across Germany, stranding passengers and forcing delays at stations. Service later resumed step by step after the outage was resolved. [apnews.com]

items.title: Germany rail network briefly halted nationwide due to IT malfunction

items.content: Rail company Deutsche Bahn had to pause train services across the country for more than two-and-a-half hours.

items.standfirst: A 41-year-old man has been arrested after Isaac Clare-Watts, 26, found at Nine Ladies monument on Monday A 26-year-old man who died in a suspected murder during a summer solstice event at a Bronze Age stone circle has been named by police.Isaac Clare-Watts, from Nottingham, was found at the Nine Ladies stone circle in Stanton Lees in the Peak District in Derbyshire at about 1.38pm on Monday. clusters.title: Pakistan police rescue French woman and children allegedly held captive by husband for 12 years
clusters.neutral_headline: French woman allegedly held captive by husband for 12 years rescued in Pakistan
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC World

items.title: Pakistan police rescue French woman and children allegedly held captive by husband for 12 years
items.standfirst: Husband arrested after Sylvie Yasmina, 54, and five children found at home in north-western provincePakistan police say they have rescued a French woman and her five children after she told authorities she had been held captive by her husband for more than a decade and subjected to years of domestic abuse in the country’s north-west.The woman, identified as 54-year-old Sylvie Yasmina, was rescued earlier this week from a mud-brick home in Bara, a town in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province near the Afghan border, the district police chief, Waqar Ahmad, said. Continue reading...
items.content: Husband arrested after Sylvie Yasmina, 54, and five children found at home in north-western provincePakistan police say they have rescued a French woman and her five children after she told authorities she had been held captive by her husband for more than a decade and subjected to years of domestic abuse in the country’s north-west.The woman, identified as 54-year-old Sylvie Yasmina, was rescued earlier this week from a mud-brick home in Bara, a town in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province near the Afghan border, the district police chief, Waqar Ahmad, said. Continue reading...

items.title: French woman allegedly held captive by husband for 12 years rescued in Pakistan
items.standfirst: Yasmina alleges she was held along with her five children, and they were all cut off from the outside world.
items.content: Yasmina alleges she was held along with her five children, and they were all cut off from the outside world.

items.standfirst: Defence minister says troops not withdrawing though Tehran sees end to war in Lebanon as part of deal with USThe Israeli defence minister, Israel Katz, has said that Israeli troops would not withdraw from southern Lebanon, further complicating Iran peace talks as fighting in Lebanon continues to be an obstacle to permanent peace.Speaking on stage in an interview in Tel Aviv, Katz said Israeli troops would remain in south Lebanon – echoing sentiments from the Israeli prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu. clusters.title: Great Britain’s grid operator pays £10m for extra power to avoid supply crunch tonight
clusters.neutral_headline: Europe’s heatwave drives electricity prices to new highs as demand soars
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, Guardian Business

items.title: Great Britain’s grid operator pays £10m for extra power to avoid supply crunch tonight
items.standfirst: Neso expected to pay millions to secure enough electricity to meet demand as households turn on fans and air conditioningGreat Britain’s grid operator is expected to pay millions to fire up gas power plants to avoid a rare summer power supply crunch on Wednesday evening as extreme heat puts pressure on the energy system.The National Energy System Operator (Neso) is expected to pay about £10m on Wednesday to secure enough electricity to meet demand as households turn on air conditioners and electric fans, according to industry data. Continue reading...
items.content: Neso expected to pay millions to secure enough electricity to meet demand as households turn on fans and air conditioningGreat Britain’s grid operator is expected to pay millions to fire up gas power plants to avoid a rare summer power supply crunch on Wednesday evening as extreme heat puts pressure on the energy system.The National Energy System Operator (Neso) is expected to pay about £10m on Wednesday to secure enough electricity to meet demand as households turn on air conditioners and electric fans, according to industry data. Continue reading...

items.title: Europe’s heatwave drives electricity prices to new highs as demand soars
items.standfirst: Great Britain has paid at least six times the normal price for imported power as millions turn on air conditioning and windfarm output sagsThe heatwave has prompted a sharp rise in electricity prices across European markets as millions turn to air conditioners and electric fans to battle record high temperatures, which have also caused a string of power plant outages across the continent.Great Britain imported electricity from Europe at more than six times the normal price on Tuesday as the high-pressure heat dome has slowed wind speeds, hitting renewable energy generation, and led to outages at multiple gas plants across the country. Continue reading...
items.content: Great Britain has paid at least six times the normal price for imported power as millions turn on air conditioning and windfarm output sagsThe heatwave has prompted a sharp rise in electricity prices across European markets as millions turn to air conditioners and electric fans to battle record high temperatures, which have also caused a string of power plant outages across the continent.Great Britain imported electricity from Europe at more than six times the normal price on Tuesday as the high-pressure heat dome has slowed wind speeds, hitting renewable energy generation, and led to outages at multiple gas plants across the country. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Trump says he’s ordered investigation into oil companies over alleged price gouging
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump accuses oil companies of gas price ‘gouging,’ calls for DOJ probe
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian Business, NBC World

items.title: Trump says he’s ordered investigation into oil companies over alleged price gouging
items.standfirst: Trump says he instructed justice department to investigate oil firms over high gas prices amid Middle East conflictDonald Trump said on Wednesday that he had instructed the US Department of Justice to investigate oil companies for alleged price gouging, accusing them of not lowering gas prices enough amid conflict in the Middle East.“The big oil companies are not dropping their price at the pump commensurate with the sharply lower prices they are paying for oil. Those prices are dropping like a rock! In other words, customers are being ‘gouged.’ I have instructed the DOJ to immediately start looking into this,” Trump wrote in a social media post late on Tuesday night. “Gasoline prices better start going down a lot faster than what I’m seeing!” Continue reading...
items.content: Trump says he instructed justice department to investigate oil firms over high gas prices amid Middle East conflictDonald Trump said on Wednesday that he had instructed the US Department of Justice to investigate oil companies for alleged price gouging, accusing them of not lowering gas prices enough amid conflict in the Middle East.“The big oil companies are not dropping their price at the pump commensurate with the sharply lower prices they are paying for oil. Those prices are dropping like a rock! In other words, customers are being ‘gouged.’ I have instructed the DOJ to immediately start looking into this,” Trump wrote in a social media post late on Tuesday night. “Gasoline prices better start going down a lot faster than what I’m seeing!” Continue reading...

items.title: Trump accuses oil companies of gas price ‘gouging,’ calls for DOJ probe
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump said Wednesday that he had instructed the Justice Department to immediately probe oil companies for not lowering gas prices at the pump in line with falling costs, accusing them of “gouging” consumers.
items.content: President Donald Trump said Wednesday that he had instructed the Justice Department to immediately probe oil companies for not lowering gas prices at the pump in line with falling costs, accusing them of “gouging” consumers.
